tldr: Move from Stripe (usage 2.9% + $0.30) to Adyen (interchange++ ~EUR 0.11 + scheme fee). Both are PCI-compliant PSPs, so card vault data can be transferred PCI-to-PCI. Plan 4-10 weeks of engineering: rewrite checkout, re-tokenize cards, rebuild webhook handlers, renegotiate acquiring. Best fit when TPV is high enough that lower per-transaction cost outweighs Stripe's faster DX. Consult official docs for current procedures.
why_switch: Lower effective rate at scale: interchange++ vs blended 2.9% + $0.30
Single global acquirer across EU/US/APAC reduces FX and cross-border fees
Unified online + in-store (POS) processing on one platform
Direct access to 250+ local payment methods without third-party connectors
Stronger enterprise SLAs and dedicated account management
Richer raw data (network tokens, settlement detail) for finance and risk teams
what_to_export: Customer records (Stripe customer IDs, email, metadata)
Saved payment methods / cards (pm_ and card_ tokens via PCI export)
Active subscriptions and their billing anchors / schedules
Open invoices and payment intents in non-terminal states
Historical charge / refund / dispute records (for reporting and chargebacks)
Webhook endpoint URLs and signing secrets
Coupons, promotion codes, and tax rates
Radar rules and fraud allow/block lists
Product / price catalog used in Checkout
export_method: Request a PCI-compliant card data export from Stripe support — they transfer encrypted card vault data directly to Adyen under a tripartite data transfer agreement. For non-card data (customers, subscriptions, invoices, charges), use the Stripe API or Sigma to dump CSV/JSON, plus Dashboard CSV exports for most resources. Build a customer-to-token mapping so migrated cards land on the correct Adyen shopperReference. Allow 2-6 weeks lead time for the PCI handoff. Consult official docs for the latest export procedure.
import_method: Adyen ingests the PCI export and re-tokenizes cards into its vault, returning shopperReference / recurringDetailReference values you map back to your customer IDs. Non-card data is loaded via the Adyen Management / Checkout APIs or CSV import for recurring details. Re-implement checkout (Drop-in or Components from @adyen/adyen-web), set up Standard Notification webhooks with HMAC signing, and configure merchant accounts per region. Run both processors in parallel during cutover and validate with test transactions on migrated cards. Consult official docs for current import endpoints.

concept_mapping.note: String identifier you supply; not auto-generated
Created via PCI handoff or first-charge tokenization
Async; result delivered via notification webhook
Different signing model — verify via HMAC key, not signing secret

concept_mapping.target: No native object — orchestrate with stored tokens
concept_mapping.note: Pair with Chargebee/Recurly or roll your own cadence
Sessions API on Adyen side; different config model
Rule engine differs; rules are not portable
KYC/onboarding model differs; not a drop-in swap
Limited native tax — pair with Avalara/TaxJar

effort: 4-10 weeks for a mid-size SaaS; 2-6 months for a marketplace using Connect. Add 2-4 weeks for PCI vault transfer lead time and Adyen underwriting.
gotchas: Adyen webhooks are async — code that relied on Stripe's synchronous charge result must be refactored
No native Subscription object — billing logic stays in your app or a billing layer
HMAC notification signing differs from Stripe's signing-secret model; verification code must be rewritten
Pricing is interchange++ with negotiated scheme fees — true cost only visible after first month's invoice
Minimum monthly fees and annual contracts apply; not a fit for low-TPV merchants
Connect → Adyen for Platforms is not a drop-in migration; KYC/onboarding flows must be rebuilt
Local payment methods (iDEAL, SEPA, etc.) need separate enablement per merchant account and region
Test environment uses different card numbers and merchant accounts — full QA pass required

email.subject: Card data migration request: Stripe to Adyen
email.body:
Hi Stripe team,

We are migrating our payment processing to Adyen and would like to coordinate the offboarding.

Could you please:

1. Initiate a PCI-compliant card data export to Adyen — we will share the receiving contact and data transfer agreement separately.
2. Confirm timeline and any required forms.
3. Keep the account active in read-only mode for 90 days after cutover so we can reconcile historical charges, refunds, and disputes.
4. Provide final invoices and confirm the closure date.

We appreciate the partnership and the platform quality — this decision is driven by scale economics, not by any service issue.

Thanks,
[Your name]
